articles.title: Hezbollah reports attacks on Israeli troops across southern Lebanon
articles.summary: Hezbollah reports attacks on Israeli troops across southern Lebanon Hezbollah said its fighters carried out 22 strikes against Israeli forces in southern Lebanon and northern Israel on Saturday. The group said attacks targeted Israeli troop gatherings and vehicles in Biyyada, Rachaf, Deir Siryan and Chama using rockets, artillery and attack drones. Additional strikes were reported near Khiam, Naqoura and Tayr Harfa, according to Hezbollah statements. The group also claimed attacks on Israeli personnel near the border sites of Misgav Am and Jall el-Aalam, as well as a helipad in the Shlomi sett

articles.title: Report says Israel operated secret Iraq base during Iran war
articles.summary: Report says Israel operated secret Iraq base during Iran war The Wall Street Journal reported that Israel established a covert military outpost in the Iraqi desert to support its air campaign against Iran, citing people familiar with the matter, including US officials. According to the report, the installation housed Israeli special forces, served as a logistical hub for air operations and included search-and-rescue teams intended to assist downed pilots. The newspaper said the base was built shortly before the US-Israel war on Iran with the knowledge of the United States. The report said the

articles.title: Chalmers on 'Unacceptable' Australian Housing Status Quo
articles.summary: Australian Treasurer Jim Chalmers will hand down the 2026-27 Federal Budget on May 12. It's framed as one the country's most consequential Budgets against a backdrop of inflation-fuelling global energy shocks, climbing cost-of-living, slowing productivity and a national debt edging toward A$1 trillion. Chalmers speaks with Bloomberg's James Mayger in Canberra. (Source: Bloomberg)

He said ongoing demolitions and other Israeli measures continue to force Palestinians from their homes. Speaking to CNBC, industry leaders said the closure of the Strait of Hormuz has laid bare how vulnerable global supply chains are to shocks. They warned the disruption will drive a fundamental shift in the energy landscape, with governments and companies expected to place far greater emphasis on resilience. articles.title: Latmiya: Inside the Ashura rituals shaping Iran’s wartime narrative
articles.summary: Latmiya: Inside the Ashura rituals shaping Iran’s wartime narrative In darkened halls across  Iran , hundreds of men dressed in black beat their chests in unison as religious eulogists chant rhythmic laments of martyrdom, sacrifice and war. The performances, often lit in red and shared widely on YouTube and social media in Iran, have become a recurring feature of the country's wartime atmosphere since the 12-day Iran-Israel war in June 2025. The Palestinian Red Crescent Society said its teams treated two women wounded in the assault, including a 35-year-old pregnant woman and a 65-year-old woman.

articles.title: Global oil stocks drop almost 270 million barrels since start of Iran war
articles.summary: Global oil stocks drop almost 270 million barrels since start of Iran war Global oil inventories are falling sharply as the Israeli-US war on Iran constrains crude flows from the Gulf following the closure of the Strait of Hormuz, according to a Bloomberg report. The data shows stockpiles declined by about 4.8 million barrels per day between March 1 and April 25, pushing global reserves towards their lowest levels since 2018. articles.summary: Qatari LNG tanker heads to Strait of Hormuz in first transit since Iran war began A Qatari liquefied natural gas tanker is approaching the Strait of Hormuz, potentially becoming the first of its kind to pass through the waterway since the war on Iran began. Shipping data from LSEG shows the Al Kharaitiyat departed Ras Laffan and is sailing towards the strait en route to Port Qasim in Pakistan. If it completes the journey, it would mark the first known transit by a Qatari LNG tanker through the Strait of Hormuz since the start of the US-Israeli war on Iran. articles.title: Israeli attacks kill 18 in Lebanon, 3 Israeli soldiers injured
articles.summary: Israeli attacks kill 18 in Lebanon, 3 Israeli soldiers injured The Israeli army has killed at least 18 people in Lebanon so far on Saturday in a series of air strikes, despite the three-week ceasefire arrangement. Two people were killed in Nabatieh - a Syrian man and his 12-year-old daughter - three in Nahrain, three in Saadiyat, three in Haboush and seven in Saksakiyeh, including a child. Three Israeli soldiers were also wounded by a Hezbollah drone that struck northern Israel near the Lebanese border, according to the Israeli military.

articles.title: Why New Zealanders Are Moving To Australia in Record Numbers
articles.summary: About 41,000 New Zealanders moved to Australia in 2025, the most in 12 years, drawn by a more robust jobs market. When even former Prime Minister Jacinda Ardern left, it made international headlines. Net citizen migration is at its lowest since records began, and unlike in past waves, people aren't coming home. Former PM John Key, Cambridge Provost Gillian Tett, and a young lawyer who says he doubled his salary by leaving explain what's gone wrong and what it would take to reverse it. (Source: Bloomberg)
